steps.title: Rewrite the equation and understand absolute value behavior
steps.content:
Divide both sides of $2|x - 5| = k$ by 2:
$$|x - 5| = \frac{k}{2}$$
For any absolute value equation $|A| = c$:
  • If $c > 0$: we get two solutions (e.g., $x - 5 = c$ and $x - 5 = -c$)
  • If $c = 0$: we get exactly one solution ($A = 0$ only)
  • If $c < 0$: we get no solutions (absolute value is never negative)

steps.title: Find the value that gives exactly one solution
steps.content:
For the equation $|x - 5| = \frac{k}{2}$ to have exactly one solution, we need:
$$\frac{k}{2} = 0$$
This gives $|x - 5| = 0$, which has exactly one solution: $x = 5$. The only possible value for $\frac{k}{2}$ is \$0$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
